purpose: Qualify two frozen, architecture-distinct MatPES-PBE-2025.2 teachers for later offline PES supervision; this protocol does not authorize H1 or online sampling guidance.

dataset.name: MatPES-PBE-2025.2
dataset.stress_source_unit: kbar Voigt [xx,yy,zz,yz,xz,xy] with DFT compressive-positive sign

selection.method: take the lexicographically smallest SHA256(protocol + ':' + matpes_id) values from the complete held-out test split

qualification_rules: both pinned checkpoint snapshots have complete file hashes and load without fallback
the two model classes and weight hashes are distinct
both checkpoints declare the MatPES-PBE-2025.2 energy reference, cutoff and periodic neighbor convention
the primary TensorNet exposes a named atom-feature layer suitable for frozen offline representation extraction
held-out energy, force and stress MAEs satisfy their separately frozen limits
translation, proper-rotation, atom-permutation and unimodular-cell invariance/covariance satisfy the frozen FP32 limits
all predictions are finite and no selected structure fails
teacher disagreement is nonzero and is retained as an uncertainty field, not used as reverse-sampling guidance
advancement_rule: H0-C qualifies only if every rule passes. H0-D remains unauthorized until H0-C is committed and pushed; H1 remains unauthorized until H0-A through H0-E all qualify.
